---
title: "rst_to_md"
author: "Sanne Govaert"
date: "2026-05-13"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Setup

### Select file to convert

### Select package

For what package do you want to (re)generate the documentation? See `rst_files.yml` for settings:

```{r}
package <- "b3alien"
```

### Load libraries

```{r}
library(here)
library(rmarkdown)
library(fs)
library(purrr)
```

### Read yaml

```{r}
rst_yaml <- yaml::read_yaml(here::here("src", "external", "rst_files.yml"))
```

## Create documentation

### Select yaml

```{r}
yaml_package <- rst_yaml[[package]]
```

### Create interim directory and define file paths

This code works for as long as each package has only one .rst file. If there are multiple .rst files, the code needs to be adapted to loop through all urls.

```{r}
dir <- here::here("src", "external", "interim", package)
dir.create(dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Get the basename of the input rmd_file without extension.
url <- purrr::pluck(yaml_package, 1, "source")
file_name <- fs::path_file(fs::path_ext_remove(url))
  
rst_file <- file.path(dir, paste0(file_name, ".rst"))
md_file <- file.path(dir, paste0(file_name, ".md"))
```

### Download the .rst file

```{r}
download.file(url, rst_file, mode = "wb")
```

### Convert .rst to .md using pandoc

```{r}
pandoc <- rmarkdown::find_pandoc()$dir
pandoc_bin <- file.path(pandoc, "pandoc")

system2(
  pandoc_bin,
  c(rst_file, "-f", "rst", "-t", "gfm", "-o", md_file)
)
```
